search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG98 hearing aid follow-up communication needs device issue audiology
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic reprogramming, repair, replacement, implant, imaging, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Device, ear, hearing, communication, and user factors require audiology assessment.
No device change, procedure, investigation, referral, or treatment is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults using hearing aids; children, implantable devices, sudden hearing change, and severe ear symptoms require separate pathways.
setting_applicability: Community, primary, audiology, or ENT hearing-aid review.
UAE_applicability: NICE guidance requires UAE audiology licensing, device procurement, warranty, fitting, payer, ENT, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG98 audiology and signs sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: NG98 remains current and was reviewed in 2023.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact adult audiology section supports hearing and communication impact, associated or negative symptoms, patient goals, ENT assessment, existing audiology or tympanometry results, and individualized hearing-support review without selecting or programming a device.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact signs section supports clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without recommending repair, replacement, imaging, or referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

unresolved_source_gaps: Device type and side, fault, fit, comfort, use, maintenance, hearing change, ear symptoms, communication goals, examination, audiology, repair, replacement, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
